_schema.name: short id used in results table
_schema.urls: map of transport → URL; keys: raw-quic, h3-wt
_schema.disabled: bool; if true, skip this relay entirely (default false)
_schema.disabled_suites: list of interop suite names to skip for this relay

relays.name: cloudflare-d14
relays.notes: CF d14 moq-rs: publisher must send PUB_NS + PUBLISH

relays.name: moq-dev-anon
relays.notes: Subscriber-only anon endpoint: publish is rejected at QUIC (code 91141958510816), SUBSCRIBE returns non-spec code=404 Broadcast-not-found. Re-enable if a write-capable path becomes available.

relays.name: quichemoq
relays.notes: Every connect drops with bare ConnectionError (no QUIC code, no reason). Possibly not MoQT-speaking on this port. Re-probe before enabling.

relays.name: quicr-west
relays.notes: Cisco libquicr (server cert unacceptable → insecure). d14 dropped (raw + WT now unresponsive — relay appears d16-only). compat=libquicr: relay returns SUBSCRIBE_OK for a nonexistent track (deferred-delivery policy), tolerated + annotated.

relays.name: moqtail
relays.notes: OzU moqtail (Rust), WT d14 only. Disabled: WT CONNECT refused (code=0) at the catalog URL (no :path). Re-enable once the correct WebTransport path is known / relay confirmed up.

relays.name: imquic
relays.notes: Meetecho imquic (C). d14 probes rejected (WT: "No WebTransport protocol offered"; QUIC close 376); only d16 tested here.

relays.name: cf-d16-interop
relays.notes: Cloudflare moq-rs draft-16 interop branch. Upstream also advertises an H3/WT endpoint at https://.../moq but our client hits a SETUP parse error ("Read out of bounds") — QUIC only here until that's resolved.
